title: EscalationPolicy
description: Sign-off escalation matrix (#29). Per priority tier, an ordered ladder of levels the orchestrator climbs while a finalized report sits unsigned. Config/policy only -- roles, channels, timings; never PHI. The orchestrator owns the durable clock (Temporal) and maps each fired level onto a comms.dispatch; targetRole is resolved to a real recipient downstream (RIS on-call schedule / Communications Agent).

defaultTier.description: Tier key applied when a study's priorityTier is unknown or missing. Must be a key of `tiers` (enforced by validate_contracts.py).

tiers.description: Escalation ladder keyed by priority tier (e.g. STAT, URGENT, ROUTINE).

levels.description: Rungs in ascending order. `level` and `afterMinutes` must both strictly increase; at most one level may set `repeat`, and it must be the last (all enforced by validate_contracts.py).

dispatchEscalation.description: The `escalation` slice the orchestrator passes forward in a comms.dispatch input when a ladder rung fires: the rung's who/how/how-loudly plus dispatch context (scheduling fields stay internal to the orchestrator). Policy + IDs only -- never PHI. Not referenced by this document's own structure; it contracts the pass-forward payload shape.

attempt.description: 1-based fire count of this rung; >1 only when a repeating final rung re-fires.

reason.description: Human-readable cause, e.g. 'sign-off gate timed out awaiting radiologist'.

overrideUrl.description: Where the paged human releases this study's sign-off gate (#57): the authenticated override endpoint, workflowId already substituted. Optional -- omitted when SIGNOFF_OVERRIDE_URL is unset (the dev/compose default). A page that says 'act' without saying HOW is how a study sits at the gate until its ladder runs out.

level.description: 1-based rung; strictly increasing within a tier.

afterMinutes.description: Minutes from gate entry (AWAITING_SIGNOFF) at which this rung fires. Strictly increasing within a tier.

targetRole.description: Who to reach at this rung. A role, not a person -- resolved to a recipient by the RIS on-call schedule / Communications Agent.

channels.description: Delivery channels for this rung, requested via comms.dispatch.

repeat.description: If true (final rung only), keep re-firing this rung until the sign-off lands.

repeatEveryMinutes.description: Re-fire cadence when `repeat` is true; required in that case, ignored otherwise.
